name: In-Flight Transaction Manager
summary: Extends CachedUtxoIndex with pending transaction registration, confirmation/rollback lifecycle, and downstream event notification
activities: Ingest submitted tx CBOR and decode via existing decodeTx pipeline
Mark inputs as speculatively spent (set spentInTx eagerly)
Index outputs as pending via existing indexUtxoFromOutput pipeline
Parse pending records via attached Capo (if available)
Track validity deadlines anchored to chain time
Confirm pending txs when seen in blocks during checkForNewTxns
Detect contention in processTransactionForNewUtxos when confirmed tx overwrites pending tx speculative spentInTx — record contestedByTxs on losing PendingTxEntry
Gate 1: detect expired pending txs via depth-based deadline check (provisionalDepth blocks behind lastBlockHeight) — transition to rollback-pending
Gate 2: execute contention-aware rollback for settled rollback-pending entries — wait for all contested txs at provisionalDepth before proceeding
Emit txConfirmed/txRolledBack/pendingSynced events for downstream consumers
responsibilities: Owns PendingTxEntry persistence in Dexie and in-memory txd Map
Owns deadline calculation (txValidityEnd + graceBuffer vs chain time)
Owns contention detection and recording (contestedByTxs on PendingTxEntry)
Owns two-gate rollback lifecycle (rollback-pending intermediate state, contention settlement check, safe rollback execution that honors on-chain reality)
Owns pendingSyncState lifecycle (stale → fresh after first sync)
Provides synchronous isPending query against in-memory map

exclusions.why: Submission side calls registerPendingTx after successful submit; index does not submit
CapoDappProvider mediates between index events and React UI state

name: PendingTxEntry
definition: { txHash: string (PK), description: string, id: string, parentId?: string, depth: number, moreInfo?: string, txName?: string, txCborHex: string, signedTxCborHex: string, deadline: number, status: "pending" | "confirmed" | "rollback-pending" | "rolled-back", contestedByTxs?: Array<{ txHash: string, blockHeight: number }>, submittedAt: number }
discussion: Dexie-serializable record for in-flight transactions. Stores all serializable fields from TxDescription (excluding BuiltTcxStats which contains non-serializable Wallet/WalletHelper/PubKeyHash objects). Deadline is txValidityEnd + graceBuffer anchored to chain time. The in-memory Map<txHash, txd> holds the live TxDescription for same-session consumers but is not persisted. The rollback-pending status represents an intermediate state where the deadline has expired with sufficient depth but contention settlement has not yet been verified. contestedByTxs records confirmed on-chain transactions that spent UTxOs claimed by this pending tx — populated by contention detection in processTransactionForNewUtxos.

name: Submission → Index registration
description: Transaction submission side registers a successfully-submitted tx with CachedUtxoIndex by passing signed CBOR hex and serializable TxDescription fields. Called once per tx in submission order after successful network submission.
errors: Decode failure: CBOR is malformed or cannot be decoded by decodeTx()
Store failure: Dexie write fails (IndexedDB quota or corruption)

name: Index → CapoDappProvider events
description: CachedUtxoIndex emits txConfirmed, txRolledBack, and pendingSynced events via its existing EventEmitter. CapoDappProvider subscribes and updates React state. txConfirmed carries {txHash, description, txd?} for ✓ toast. txRolledBack carries {txHash, description, cbor, txd?} for recovery UI. pendingSynced signals stale→fresh transition.
trigger: Pending tx confirmed in block, deadline expires, or first sync cycle completes
errors: Event listener throws — should not propagate to index internals

name: Register Pending Transaction
description: Ingest a submitted transaction into the index as pending — decode CBOR, persist PendingTxEntry, mark inputs spent, index outputs, parse records
trigger: Submission side calls registerPendingTx(signedCborHex, opts) after successful network submission
preconditions: Transaction has been successfully submitted to the network
CachedUtxoIndex has completed initial sync (syncReady resolved)

steps.action: Decode signed CBOR via decodeTx(), extract txHash, compute deadline as txValidityEnd + graceBuffer
Persist PendingTxEntry {txHash, status:'pending', deadline, ...serializable fields} to Dexie
For each tx.body.input: find UTXO in store, set spentInTx = txHash (speculative spend)
For each tx.body.output: run through indexUtxoFromOutput(), saving UtxoIndexEntry with utxoId = txHash#idx
If Capo attached: parse inline datums from pending outputs into RecordIndexEntry via parseDelegatedDatum()
Store live TxDescription in in-memory pendingTxMap for same-session consumers
Save tx CBOR to txs table via store.saveTx() for future reference

postconditions: PendingTxEntry exists in Dexie with status 'pending'
Consumed UTXOs have spentInTx set to pending txHash
New outputs are queryable (identifiable as pending via txHash prefix in pending table)
Records parsed from pending outputs are queryable (if Capo attached)

name: Confirm Pending Transaction
description: When checkForNewTxns discovers a transaction that matches a pending entry, promote it to confirmed status and notify downstream
trigger: checkForNewTxns (60s poll) discovers txHash in Blockfrost results that matches a PendingTxEntry with status pending
preconditions: PendingTxEntry exists with status pending for the discovered txHash

steps.action: During checkForNewTxns processing, check each new txHash against pending-tx table via store.findPendingTx(txHash)
Skip normal indexing — outputs already indexed, inputs already marked spent
Set PendingTxEntry status to 'confirmed' via store.setPendingTxStatus(txHash, 'confirmed')
Emit txConfirmed event {txHash, description, txd?} — txd available from in-memory map if same session

postconditions: UTXOs and records remain unchanged (already correct)

name: Contention-Aware Two-Gate Rollback
description: When a pending tx fails to confirm, rolls back speculative state through a two-gate lifecycle that accounts for contention — where a confirmed on-chain tx consumed inputs the pending tx also claimed. Gate 1 detects expiry with sufficient chain depth (deadline provisionalDepth blocks behind lastBlockHeight). Gate 2 waits for contention evidence to settle (all competing confirmed txs at provisionalDepth) before executing the actual rollback.
trigger: Two triggers feed into the lifecycle: checkPendingDeadlines (10s timer) for Gate 1 expiry detection; rollback settlement executor (each sync cycle) for Gate 2 contention settlement
preconditions: PendingTxEntry exists with status pending and deadline slot is at least confirmationThresholds.provisionalDepth blocks behind lastBlockHeight
Contention (if any) has been recorded on the PendingTxEntry via contestedByTxs during processTransactionForNewUtxos

steps.action: Gate 1 — Expiry depth check: checkPendingDeadlines queries pending entries whose deadlineSlot is at least confirmationThresholds.provisionalDepth blocks behind lastBlockHeight — ensuring the chain has moved past the deadline with enough depth that the deadline block itself is unlikely to be rolled back. Sets status to rollback-pending. Does NOT execute rollback.

Gate 2 — Contention settlement: Rollback executor (runs each sync cycle) queries rollback-pending entries. For each: if contestedByTxs is non-empty, checks that all competing txs have lastBlockHeight - blockHeight >= confirmationThresholds.provisionalDepth. If any are too shallow, skips (re-check next cycle). When clear (or no contention), proceeds to rollback execution steps 3-9.

Safe spentInTx clearing: clearSpentByTx(txHash) only finds UTxOs still marked spentInTx === txHash. Contested inputs were already overwritten to the confirmed tx hash at detection time in processTransactionForNewUtxos. Clears only uncontested inputs — safe by construction.
Delete pending-origin UTxOs: remove UTxOs where utxoId starts with txHash#. Confirmed tx outputs have different tx hashes and are unaffected.
Delete pending-origin records: remove records where utxoId starts with txHash#. Same rationale as step 4.

Selective datum re-parse: re-parse inline datums ONLY for input UTxOs whose spentInTx was cleared in step 3 (uncontested inputs). Do NOT re-parse inputs whose spentInTx points to a confirmed tx — those were legitimately spent, successor records already correctly indexed by processTransactionForNewUtxos. Requires Capo attached; no-op without Capo.
artifacts: records table rows recreated from restored UTxOs

steps.action: Set PendingTxEntry status to rolled-back.
Emit txRolledBack event {txHash, description, cbor, txd?} — cbor included for recovery path, txd from memory if available.
In-memory cleanup: remove from pendingTxMap and pendingSpentUtxoIds.

postconditions: Contested inputs retain their confirmed tx s spentInTx (on-chain reality preserved)
Pending outputs and records removed from store
Records from restored (uncontested) inputs recreated via datum re-parse (if Capo attached)
CapoDappProvider receives txRolledBack event for recovery UI

name: Startup Recovery
description: On page reload, load pending state from Dexie, expose as stale, then resolve actual status after first sync
trigger: CachedUtxoIndex construction / syncNow on page reload
preconditions: PendingTxEntry rows may exist in Dexie from previous session

steps.action: Load PendingTxEntry rows with status='pending' from Dexie. Set pendingSyncState to 'stale'. Consumers can query getPendingTxs() immediately.
Run checkForNewTxns() — discovers which pending txs landed in blocks during offline period. Confirms those via normal confirmation flow.
Run checkPendingDeadlines() — rolls back any pending txs whose deadline passed during offline period.
Flip pendingSyncState to 'fresh'. Emit pendingSynced event.
Note: in-memory pendingTxMap is empty after reload — events fire without live txd. Consumers get txHash, description, cbor from Dexie fields.

postconditions: All pending entries have verified status (confirmed, rolled-back, or still pending with valid deadline)

name: Pending transactions
comment: PendingTxEntry Dexie table tracking in-flight transaction lifecycle. Cross-cutting: affects query behavior across all UTXO/record query methods, drives events consumed by CapoDappProvider.

name: Validity deadline tracking
comment: 10s timer comparing pending tx deadline slots against chain tip depth. Transitions to rollback-pending only when the deadline is confirmationThresholds.provisionalDepth blocks behind lastBlockHeight — not on raw slot comparison. This prevents premature rollback from shallow blocks, clock drift, or stale sync. Same provisionalDepth threshold used by Gate 2 (contention settlement).

name: Tx confirmation/rollback events
comment: txConfirmed, txRolledBack, pendingSynced events emitted via existing EventEmitter. Consumed by CapoDappProvider for ✓ toast, recovery UI, and stale→fresh state transitions. txRolledBack includes cbor and txd for recovery path.

question: What test strategy should be used for pending-tx features?
context: Current CachedUtxoIndex tests are all Blockfrost/preprod-based with real API keys. Pending-tx features need either emulator-based test infrastructure for CachedUtxoIndex or integration tests with real preprod submissions.

question: Are there edge cases with rapid sequential chained transaction submissions?
context: Per-tx registration with distinct txHashes handles chains naturally (txB rollback doesn't affect txA). But rapid submission of chained txs where txB spends a pending output of txA, and txA gets rolled back, requires that txB's rollback also fires. The current model may handle this implicitly (txA rollback deletes its outputs, which are txB's inputs, but txB's spentInTx references may not get cleaned). Needs analysis.

content: Key design decisions made during interview: (1) CBOR as integration format — no Helios coupling leak. (2) No new fields on UtxoIndexEntry/RecordIndexEntry — pending state is a tx property, not UTXO property. (3) Reuse spentInTx for speculative spends — pending-tx table determines if spend is speculative. (4) isPending on CachedUtxoIndex not FoundDatumUtxo — keeps FoundDatumUtxo as plain type. (5) BuiltTcxStats excluded from persistence — contains non-serializable Wallet/WalletHelper. (6) Deadline anchored to chain time not wallclock — prevents premature rollback. (7) Register after successful submit, not before. (8) CapoDappProvider mediates all UI access. (9) 72h purge retention. (10) 10s deadline timer separate from 60s Blockfrost poll.

name: Contention detection and settlement
comment: When processTransactionForNewUtxos discovers a confirmed tx spending a UTxO already claimed by a pending tx, it overwrites spentInTx with the confirmed tx hash (on-chain reality wins) and records the contention on the losing PendingTxEntry via contestedByTxs. This makes subsequent rollback safe by construction: clearSpentByTx only finds uncontested inputs. Contention settlement (Gate 2) waits for all competing txs to reach provisionalDepth before executing rollback.
